synthesis.executive_summary: SwarmMesh does NOT exist in the Terminal Stampede codebase. Zero references found. The project uses filesystem-based IPC with atomic operations, not mesh networking.

key_findings.finding: No SwarmMesh references exist in any file. Search for 'SwarmMesh', 'swarm-mesh', 'swarm_mesh', and 'mesh' in swarm context returned zero matches.
Uses filesystem-based IPC only. No mesh networking, sockets, or peer-to-peer discovery. Has a 'collaboration bus' for metaswarm with append-only JSONL ledgers in collab/ directory.
No mesh mentions. Documents central orchestrator + filesystem IPC model with optional hierarchical metaswarm (50 Squad Leads × 5 Workers = 250 per commander).
Zero documentation for SwarmMesh. No design specs, no scaffold templates, no mesh architecture docs. Documentation readiness is 0%.
Current IPC mechanisms (atomic file moves, JSONL ledgers, commander heartbeats) are mesh-friendly for shared filesystem scenarios but require transport layer for cross-host mesh.

current_architecture.task_distribution: Atomic file mv from queue/ to claimed/
current_architecture.coordination: Append-only JSONL ledgers in collab/ (metaswarm only)

scaffold_readiness_assessment.shared_fs_mesh_readiness: HIGH - existing patterns (atomic moves, JSONL ledgers, heartbeats) work if all agents see same directory
scaffold_readiness_assessment.network_mesh_readiness: LOW - requires new transport layer, membership discovery, distributed heartbeats

gaps_for_swarmmesh: No transport layer for cross-host communication
reusable_patterns: queue/claim/results contract with atomic rename semantics

conclusion: SwarmMesh is not implemented, referenced, or planned in Terminal Stampede. The existing filesystem-based architecture has high readiness for mesh over shared storage but low readiness for network-based mesh without significant new infrastructure.
